stocks.name: Brookfield Asset Management
stocks.thesis: Record Q4: FRE US$867M (+28%), US$112B fundraised 2025, $100B AI infrastructure program, $1T AUM. 15% dividend hike. Connor Teskey named CEO. Analyst target C$87-104 (26-50% upside).
stocks.risks: Premium valuation. AUM growth dependent on fundraising environment. Complexity of Brookfield ecosystem.

stocks.name: Alaris Equity Partners
stocks.thesis: Growing royalty structure. Diversified private company portfolio. High yield with growth potential.
stocks.risks: Above historical averages — better entry ~$20. Private company exposure is opaque. Small cap liquidity.

stocks.name: Intact Financial
stocks.thesis: Record FY2025: NOIPS C$19.21 (+33%), EPS C$18.35 (+48%), ROE 19.5%. 11% dividend hike, 21 consecutive years, 10% CAGR. Analyst consensus C$317.91 (20% upside). Ex-div March 17.
stocks.risks: P&C insurance cycle risk. CAT loss exposure. Below 52-week high by 17%.

stocks.name: Canadian Apartment Properties REIT
stocks.thesis: Canada largest residential REIT at NEW 52-week low. 37.5% discount to NAV of C$56.41. 45,500 suites. FFO/unit C$2.541. Monthly payer. $294M in NCIB buybacks at discount to NAV. Housing supply crisis tailwind. Every analyst target above current price.
stocks.risks: Interest rate sensitivity. Softer 2026 rental market. REIT distributions less tax-efficient than eligible dividends.

stocks.name: Dream Industrial REIT
stocks.thesis: Industrial REIT below 5-year average after rate-hike drawdown. 3 analyst upgrades in 10 days. Avg target C$14.82 (11% upside + yield). Warehouse/logistics demand secular tailwind.
stocks.risks: Drew bearish on RE broadly. Interest rate sensitivity. Industrial REIT supply risk.

stocks.name: TELUS Corporation
stocks.thesis: 5G capex tapering driving FCF surge — record $2.2B FCF in 2025, up 11% YoY with 10%+ CAGR guided through 2028. Committed to 3-8% annual dividend increases 2026-2028. Near 52-week lows.
stocks.risks: High debt load. TELUS International drag. CRTC regulatory risk. Dividend growth may be lower end of range.

stocks.name: Magna International
stocks.thesis: North America's largest auto parts supplier. 20% below 52-week high. 9.8x normalized P/E. 15 consecutive annual dividend increases. Trump NA manufacturing policy tailwind. 350+ NA facilities.
stocks.risks: Auto production is cyclical. Cross-border tariff complexity. EV transition capex.

stocks.name: Granite Real Estate Investment Trust
stocks.thesis: Industrial REIT pulled back 14% from ATH to below 200-day MA. BMO target C$97, TD C$96. Monthly payer. Revenue C$618.7M, net income C$342M. ~19% discount to estimated NAV of C$99. E-commerce/logistics secular tailwind.
stocks.risks: Interest rate sensitivity. Industrial supply growth could pressure rents. P/E 15.9x is mid-range.

stocks.name: Brookfield Infrastructure Partners
stocks.thesis: Record 2025: FFO US$2.6B (+6%), net income +179%. 17th consecutive distribution increase (+6%). Data segment surged 50% — AI infrastructure growth engine with 3.6 GW pipeline. Pulled back 10% from ATH, 14% upside to analyst avg target plus 5.1% yield.
stocks.risks: Complex LP structure with FX exposure across 5 continents. Interest rate sensitivity on leverage.

stocks.name: Exchange Income Corporation
stocks.thesis: Aerospace/aviation + manufacturing conglomerate. Monthly payer at C$0.23/mo. Q4 2025 beat: EPS C$1.06 vs C$1.01 est, revenue C$929M +35% YoY. Analyst target C$129 — 26% upside. Wait for pullback to C$95-98 for better entry.
stocks.risks: P/E 32.9x is elevated. Cyclical aerospace exposure. Capital-intensive fleet upgrades.

stocks.name: Manulife Financial Corp
stocks.thesis: Record FY2025 core earnings C$7.5B, core EPS C$4.21 (+8% YoY). Q4 EPS $1.12 beat $0.76 est. 10.2% dividend hike. Asia double-digit growth. 42M share buyback. 15.3x P/E on accelerating earnings — pulled back 11% from highs.
stocks.risks: Insurance cycle risk. Asia geopolitical exposure (China/HK). Broad market selloff could test C$45 support.

stocks.name: Russel Metals
stocks.thesis: PEG 0.59. Kloeckner acquisition catalyst — transformative deal. Q4 revenue $4.6B. Industrial steel distributor with solid fundamentals. Analyst target C$54 (13-40% upside).
stocks.risks: Cyclical industrial sector. Steel price volatility. Integration risk from Kloeckner acquisition.

stocks.name: RioCan REIT
stocks.thesis: Grocery-anchored, 3 analyst upgrades, active buyback program. Canada's largest REIT. Urban mixed-use development pipeline.
stocks.risks: Drew bearish on RE. Interest rate sensitivity. Retail apocalypse concerns (mitigated by grocery anchors).

stocks.name: Capital Power
stocks.thesis: Edmonton-based utility. 6% dividend hike. Data center power demand tailwind. Stable regulated earnings.
stocks.risks: Transition from coal/gas to renewables requires capex. Alberta regulatory environment.

stocks.name: Bank of Nova Scotia
stocks.thesis: Q1 2026 beat: +132% profit YoY. Pacific Alliance (LatAm) diversification is contrarian value play. Add on pullback to $98-101 for 4.4-4.5% yield.
stocks.risks: Higher international credit exposure (LatAm). Not a core sleep-at-night holding at full weight.

stocks.name: Algonquin Power & Utilities
stocks.thesis: Post-restructuring play. Sold Liberty Utilities stake. Renewable energy focus.
stocks.risks: Dividend was cut. Management credibility issues. Restructuring uncertainty.

stocks.name: Mullen Group
stocks.thesis: Diversified trucking/logistics. Solid yield. Steady cash flow generator in Canadian transport.
stocks.risks: Cyclical transport sector. Western Canadian economic exposure.

stocks.name: Canadian Pacific Kansas City
stocks.thesis: Tri-national railway post-KCS merger. Only single-line rail network connecting Canada, US, and Mexico. Synergy realization ongoing. Infrastructure moat.
stocks.risks: Low yield. High valuation. Integration risk. Trade policy sensitivity for cross-border rail.

stocks.name: Sun Life Financial Inc.
stocks.thesis: Record Q4/FY2025: underlying EPS C$7.45 (+12%), ROE 19.1%, AUM C$1.605T. 9% pullback from ATH on market weakness — not company-specific. 14x P/E for 12% growth + 47% payout ratio is cheap. Avg analyst target C$96.44 (12% upside).
stocks.risks: MFS equity market exposure. Financial leverage ratio increased to 23.5%. Insurance cycle risk.

stocks.name: Bank of Montreal
stocks.thesis: Bank of the West integration complete. Record Q1 2026 earnings (EPS C$3.48, +15% beat). Forward P/E ~14x. Cheapest Big 5 bank with best growth trajectory. Dividend raised 5% to C$6.68/year.
stocks.risks: US commercial real estate exposure. Integration costs still flowing through. Bank sector rate sensitivity.

stocks.name: Rogers Communications
stocks.thesis: 37% analyst upside. Shaw integration synergies flowing through. Wireless subscriber growth. Improved balance sheet trajectory.
stocks.risks: Heavy debt from Shaw acquisition. CRTC regulatory risk. Competitive Canadian telecom market.

stocks.name: CIBC
stocks.thesis: Record Q1 2026 revenue. Canadian bank with improving US presence. Solid dividend history. Wait for pullback to C$125.
stocks.risks: US mortgage exposure. Smaller international footprint vs peers.

stocks.name: Enghouse Systems
stocks.thesis: $269M cash, zero debt, 10.2x P/E vs 25x sector avg. Down 48% from highs. Acquisition-dependent growth model. Analyst target C$22-28 (25-57% upside).
stocks.risks: Acquisition-dependent. Below 50-day MA. Software, not infrastructure. Speculative swing trade.

stocks.name: TD Bank
stocks.thesis: C$135, 11.7x P/E. Q1 2026 beat. Post-AML recovery play. Canada's largest bank by branch count. US growth engine.
stocks.risks: AML enforcement overhang. US regulatory scrutiny. Growth cap on US acquisitions.

stocks.name: Power Corporation
stocks.thesis: Great-West Lifeco record earnings. NAV discount narrowing. 10% pullback created entry window. Holding company discount to underlying assets.
stocks.risks: Holding company discount may persist. Complex corporate structure. Insurance cycle exposure.

stocks.name: IGM Financial
stocks.thesis: Wealth management platform. Power Corp subsidiary. AUM growth catalyst. Cheap valuation relative to AUM.
stocks.risks: Fee compression in wealth management. Passive investing trend. Power Corp governance.

stocks.name: Crombie REIT
stocks.thesis: Grocery-anchored REIT (Sobeys/Empire). 27% discount to NAV. 98.6% occupancy. 84% AFFO payout ratio. Defensive retail exposure.
stocks.risks: Drew bearish on RE. Interest rate sensitivity. Concentrated tenant exposure (Empire/Sobeys).

stocks.name: Allied Properties REIT
stocks.thesis: Office distress play. Potential recovery if return-to-office accelerates. Deep value at current levels.
stocks.risks: Office sector headwinds. Drew bearish on RE. High risk. Cut dividend 60% in Dec 2025.

stocks.name: BCE Inc.
stocks.thesis: 56% dividend cut. Thesis broken. Do not buy.
stocks.risks: Dividend cut destroyed income thesis. Heavy debt. Declining legacy business.

stocks.name: Great-West Lifeco
stocks.thesis: Record earnings. 10% dividend hike. Insurance/wealth management conglomerate. Strong capital position. POW.TO subsidiary with direct earnings exposure.
stocks.risks: Insurance cycle risk. Interest rate sensitivity. Complex corporate structure under Power Corp.

stocks.name: North West Company
stocks.thesis: Defensive retailer serving remote/northern Canadian communities. Near-monopoly in many markets. Steady cash flows. Recession-resistant.
stocks.risks: Limited growth potential. Remote logistics costs. Government subsidy dependency for some markets.

stocks.name: SmartCentres REIT
stocks.thesis: Canada largest Walmart-anchored REIT. 98.6% occupancy, 35.6M sqft, C$12.1B assets. Monthly 6.8% yield. 3,500 acres owned land = development optionality.
stocks.risks: Near 52w high — limited upside to analyst targets. Interest rate sensitivity. Retail REIT secular concerns.

stocks.name: National Bank of Canada
stocks.thesis: Fastest growing Canadian bank. Strong Quebec market dominance. CWB acquisition adds Western Canadian presence.
stocks.risks: Smaller scale vs Big 5. Quebec concentration risk. CWB integration.

stocks.name: Cogeco Communications
stocks.thesis: 44% intrinsic value discount per Morningstar 5-star rating. Fiber buildout catalyst. Undervalued Canadian telecom/cable.
stocks.risks: Cable cord-cutting secular trend. Smaller player vs BCE/Rogers.

stocks.name: CT Real Estate Investment Trust
stocks.thesis: Canadian Tire-anchored retail REIT. 5.5% yield with 11 consecutive annual distribution increases. Robust AFFO growth and conservative payout ratio. At consensus target — wait for dip to C$15.50.
stocks.risks: At analyst consensus with limited upside. Retail REIT secular concerns. Interest rate sensitivity.

stocks.name: Canadian Tire
stocks.thesis: Iconic Canadian retail. Strong dividend track record. Financial services segment (Triangle credit cards) adds recurring revenue. Loyalty program moat.
stocks.risks: Consumer discretionary exposure. E-commerce competition. Canadian consumer sentiment.

stocks.name: Freehold Royalties
stocks.thesis: Pure royalty model — zero operating costs. Permian Basin record output. Monthly payer. Net debt/FFO 1.1x. Oil-leveraged income without capex drag.
stocks.risks: Payout ratio 142% is elevated. Oil price dependent. Near 52w high.

stocks.name: Nutrien Ltd.
stocks.thesis: Worlds largest fertilizer producer, benefiting from geopolitical supply disruptions driving commodity prices higher. Recent analyst upgrades and active buyback program provide tailwinds.
stocks.risks: Fertilizer prices are cyclical and volatile; a US DOJ antitrust investigation is an overhang.

stocks.name: ARC Resources
stocks.thesis: LNG Canada tailwind. 30% analyst upside. Premier Montney producer with low-cost operations.
stocks.risks: Commodity price exposure. LNG Canada delays could impact thesis.

stocks.name: Whitecap Resources
stocks.thesis: Monthly payer. 15% total shareholder return (6% production growth + 7% yield + 2% buybacks). Light oil producer with stable production growth. Near ATH — wait for 10-15% pullback to C$12.50.
stocks.risks: Near ATH. Oil price dependent. Western Canadian focused.

stocks.name: Canadian Utilities Limited
stocks.thesis: Canada's ONLY Dividend King — 53 consecutive annual dividend increases. C$12B capex backlog (2026-2030) including C$2.9B Yellowhead Pipeline (100% contracted). Alberta data center demand tailwind for electricity transmission assets. Adjusted EPS C$2.42 grew despite headwinds. Beta 0.61 — ultimate sleep-at-night stock. Best entry at C$41-43 pullback.
stocks.risks: Near 52-week high with analyst avg target below current price. 1% dividend growth is glacial. D/E 158%.

stocks.name: Enbridge Inc.
stocks.thesis: 31 consecutive dividend raises. Record 2025: GAAP earnings $7.1B, adjusted EBITDA $20B, DCF $12.5B. $39B secured backlog. 20th consecutive year meeting guidance. Best entry $65-67.
stocks.risks: D/EBITDA 4.8x. Near ATH with limited margin of safety. Best re-entry $65-67 for 5.8-6.0% yield.

stocks.name: Emera Inc.
stocks.thesis: Utility play with Tampa Electric (Florida) providing US growth. Regulated earnings base. Dividend growth resuming after pause.
stocks.risks: Hurricane exposure via Tampa Electric. Higher debt load vs FTS. Dividend was frozen (now resuming).

stocks.name: Fortis Inc.
stocks.thesis: 51-year consecutive dividend increase streak — longest in Canada. Pure regulated utility. Predictable earnings. Rate base growth 6% CAGR through 2029.
stocks.risks: Lower yield vs peers. Slow growth. Interest rate sensitivity for utilities.

stocks.name: TC Energy Corporation
stocks.thesis: Post-spinoff pure nat gas pipeline + nuclear play. 57,500mi pipeline network, 15 flow records in 2025. Data center demand exploding — Columbia Gas open season 3x oversubscribed. 26 consecutive dividend increases. C$11.6-11.8B EBITDA guided for 2026. Entry zone C$78-82.
stocks.risks: Near ATH with thin margin of safety. ~98% GAAP payout ratio. Above average analyst target. Goldman Strong Sell.

stocks.name: Quebecor Inc.
stocks.thesis: 6.5x EV/EBITDA — 25-35% discount to telecom peers. Freedom Mobile makes them Canada's 4th national carrier. ROE 37.4%. Six analyst upgrades Feb 28. Consensus C$58, top target C$63.
stocks.risks: Competitive wireless market. Debt from Freedom Mobile acquisition. Quebec market concentration.

stocks.name: Gibson Energy
stocks.thesis: Record infrastructure EBITDA. 7th consecutive dividend raise. Midstream energy with toll-based revenue model.
stocks.risks: At ATH — wait for pullback to C$25-26 for better entry. Western Canadian energy concentration.

stocks.name: Pembina Pipeline
stocks.thesis: Midstream energy — pipelines, processing, logistics. MONTHLY payer. 12+ year dividend growth streak. Diversified across Western Canadian basin. Collects tolls regardless of commodity price. Entry zone under $52.
stocks.risks: Midstream sector regulatory risk. Pipeline capacity constraints. Commodity volume sensitivity.

stocks.name: Brookfield Renewable Partners
stocks.thesis: Record 2025 FFO US$1.334B (+10% YoY). Google hydro deal (3,000 MW), Westinghouse nuclear pact with U.S. Gov't. 15 consecutive distribution increases, 5% hike Jan 2026. 10-15% FFO growth guided 2026. Data center demand = multi-decade tailwind. 16% below 52-week high despite best year ever.
stocks.risks: Complex LP structure with FX exposure. Interest rate sensitivity. Renewable energy policy risk. GAAP net loss (FFO is real metric).

stocks.name: Canadian Natural Resources
stocks.thesis: 26 consecutive dividend raises. 6.4% increase to $2.50/yr announced Mar 5/26. Q4 profit $5.3B. Oil sands moat. Low decline rate assets.
stocks.risks: Oil price exposure. ESG/climate policy risk for oil sands. Capital intensive operations.
